behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is the primary boundary: correctness depends on semantic extraction quality from a real model, and outputs can vary across runs/prompts/model versions. A pure mock or static fixture would likely pass while missing real extraction failures (MOCK-HIDE). No special OS/subprocess boundary is required beyond calling the real model API, so Workflow is not required for this single-reviewer behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor reviewer against a labeled contract set with ground-truth key terms, and assert recall/coverage per term type meets threshold; include varied phrasing to expose misses that mocks cannot reveal.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: This is model judgment quality, especially for indirect/euphemistic language, so LLM-DEP applies strongly. Mocked outputs would hide the core failure mode (MOCK-HIDE), because the difficult part is semantic interpretation under policy, not plumbing. Multi-step agent loop is not required for this single reviewer.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real risk-assessor model on a curated evaluation set containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses under a concrete policy configuration, then assert detection quality metrics (e.g., recall on risky cases and acceptable false positives).

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model reasoning over legal text and jurisdictional rules (LLM-DEP). Deterministic mocks can validate wiring but can miss misclassification of compliance status (MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE). Unless the behavior explicitly requires multi-step agent interactions, Agentic is the minimum boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use real compliance-checker calls on a jurisdiction-specific benchmark of contracts with expert-labeled compliant/non-compliant outcomes and requirement-level rationales; assert decision accuracy and requirement coverage thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says "standard" but risk assessor says "high risk"), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is schema/contract interaction between reviewer outputs and merge logic (MOCK-MISS possible if testing merge in isolation with idealized objects). Real LLM quality is not needed here; synthetic conflicting outputs are sufficient. Unit may be enough for a pure function, but Integration is safer minimum to catch cross-module serialization/field-mapping issues.
behaviors.test_description: Run merge through actual reviewer-output adapters/parsers using realistic conflicting payloads from at least two reviewer modules, and assert unified report contains both assessments, correct clause linkage, and reviewer attribution without overwrite.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full agent pipeline with multiple agentic components plus orchestration/merge. Real failures can arise from concurrent dispatch, partial collection, and cross-step state/result-loss interactions that mocks often hide (MOCK-MISS, MULTI-STEP). Because real reviewer outputs are part of acceptance, this exceeds plain System and requires Workflow-level validation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end on real contracts with all three real reviewers in parallel, then verify completion, per-reviewer output presence, and that every reviewer finding is represented in the final merged report (with provenance), across repeated runs to catch intermittent loss/race issues.
